patternCandidates.name: HARNESS.md 7-Phase SOP
patternCandidates.description: Systematic methodology for wrapping GUI apps as agent-native CLIs. 7 phases: codebase analysis → CLI architecture → implementation → backend integration → export → session → REPL + SKILL.md generation.
patternCandidates.relevance_to_home: Directly applicable to JASON-OS Domain 02a (Tool Integration).

patternCandidates.name: SKILL.md Command Catalog Format
patternCandidates.description: 37 SKILL.md files. Flat command references for agent discoverability. Different design than sonash workflow-definition SKILL.md.

patternCandidates.name: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json
patternCandidates.description: First public Claude Code plugin format reference. marketplace.json + plugin source dir.

patternCandidates.name: Registry + CLI-Hub Distribution
patternCandidates.description: registry.json (35 entries) → static hub → pip install. Agent-discoverable catalog via meta-skill.

patternCandidates.name: ReplSkin Terminal UI
patternCandidates.description: Python prompt_toolkit branded REPL. Master copy in plugin, distributed to harnesses.

patternCandidates.name: Codec Allowlist Pattern
patternCandidates.relevance_to_home: Similar to existing sonash security patterns.

patternCandidates.name: Skill Generator (Jinja2)
patternCandidates.description: Auto-generates SKILL.md from CLI introspection using Jinja2 templates.

knowledgeCandidates.name: Agent-native software methodology
knowledgeCandidates.description: Core insight: AI agents can't use GUI software, wrap it systematically. HARNESS.md is the how-to.

knowledgeCandidates.name: Build-vs-integrate decision for JASON-OS
knowledgeCandidates.description: Should JASON-OS invoke CLI-Anything harnesses (Python) or generate Node/TS wrappers using same methodology?

knowledgeCandidates.name: Quality treadmill case study
knowledgeCandidates.description: 28K stars in 29 days, 35 harnesses, 0 CI test enforcement. Growth outpacing quality gates. Opposite lesson from build-your-own-x stagnation.
knowledgeCandidates.relevance_to_home: Your quality infrastructure prevents this pattern.

contentCandidates.name: MCP Backend Pattern (guides/mcp-backend.md)
contentCandidates.relevance_to_home: Wrapping MCP servers as CLI backends. You have 3 MCP servers. Directly applicable to JASON-OS.

contentCandidates.name: Skill Auto-Generation (guides/skill-generation.md)
contentCandidates.relevance_to_home: Auto-generate SKILL.md from CLI introspection. Could inform /skill-creator automation.

contentCandidates.name: Mermaid Harness (lightest-weight example)
contentCandidates.relevance_to_home: No local binary required. Ready to use for agent-driven diagram generation.

contentCandidates.name: Exa Harness (AI search alternative)
contentCandidates.relevance_to_home: Structured content extraction. Alternative/complement for /deep-research searchers.

antiPatternCandidates.name: No CI for tests pattern
antiPatternCandidates.description: 74 test files, 1839+ tests, zero CI enforcement. Tests exist for documentation, not quality gates. Don't regress on your pre-commit/CI enforcement if adopting patterns.
antiPatternCandidates.relevance_to_home: Your pre-commit hooks and pattern checker ARE the enforcement CLI-Anything lacks.

antiPatternCandidates.name: Monorepo without shared test infrastructure
antiPatternCandidates.description: 35 independent packages, 0 conftest.py, no shared runner. A change to repl_skin.py can't be verified against 35 consumers. If JASON-OS distributes skills as packages, plan shared testing.
antiPatternCandidates.relevance_to_home: Skill testing strategy if JASON-OS distributes.

antiPatternCandidates.name: Growing faster than quality gates
antiPatternCandidates.description: 28K stars in 29 days with 30+ contributors and no automated quality enforcement. Same trajectory as build-your-own-x stagnation, just faster.
antiPatternCandidates.relevance_to_home: Your quality infrastructure prevents this. Don't remove it.

cross_repo_connections.detail: notebooklm harness wraps notebooklm-py CLI. Build-vs-integrate decision point.
CLI-Anything wraps existing software; build-your-own-x teaches building from scratch. Opposite approaches to 'understanding systems'.
